programmes.show_description: VW T25 / Audi TT Quattro: The team pay over the odds for a VW Campervan, but can they hope to break even when they turn out a lovely bus for auction.
Cortina/Granada: Dave travels to leafy Leicestershire where Steve Perry has a 1980 Ford Granada Ghia and a 1981 Ford Cortina.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in East Sussex and Hertfordshire, with finds that include a Victorian egg incubator.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Destination Unknown: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha's mysterious disappearance in December 1926 and reveals the influence this episode had on her writing.
Shopping from Home.
Wakefield to Leeds: In Wakefield, West Yorkshire, Michael Portillo visits the National Coal Mining Museum for England at Caphouse Colliery.
Bradford to Hebdon Bridge: Michael Portillo continues his post-war exploration of northwest England in Bradford and Shipley, before ending at Hebden Bridge.
Tilbury to Barbican: Michael Portillo travels from London's Docklands to the Barbican, examining the story of the arrival at Tilbury in 1948 of over a thousand passengers from the Caribbean.
Waterloo to Regent's Park: Michael Portillo visits London's South Bank, where the 1951 Festival of Britain drew more than eight and a half million visitors.
Ford Fiesta/Malton Forest Rally: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i that's not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.
Robin Reliant/Triumphs/Bantam: Dave meets the inspirational owner of a Reliant Robin who's selling it to raise money for charity.
Banzai!: December 7, 1941 saw the infamous Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor. Laurence Olivier revisits this pivotal moment of World War II in the much-praised documentary series.
The Germans hear that Polish forces are fighting alongside a huge bear. Tales of this beer-swilling, chain-smoking beast terrify them - but incredibly the stories are true.
Nursing Special: Fiona Bruce presents an edition dedicated to nursing, with objects providing an insight into the history of the profession.
Eden Project 1: Fiona Bruce presents an episode filmed before the Accession, featuring the Queen Consort's visit to the Eden Project to discuss charity project The Big Lunch.

Mini Cooper Mk2 / MGTF: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window.
Morgan / Raleigh Super Grifter: The Restoring Classics team set out on a Pre-War challenge with a 1939 three wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on.

The Germans hear that Polish forces are fighting alongside a huge bear. Tales of this beer-swilling, chain-smoking beast terrify them - but incredibly the stories are true.
Nursing Special: Fiona Bruce presents an edition dedicated to nursing, with objects providing an insight into the history of the profession.
Simon Reeve embarks on the final leg of his journey around the Mediterranean, travelling from Morocco, across the sea to Spain, and on to Corsica and the French Riviera.
Giant Royal Locomotive: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move The Royal Highland Fusilier halfway across the country to the Great Central Railway's Diesel Gala in Loughborough.
David Jason operates one of the world's biggest hydraulic cranes, gets behind the wheel of a tank, and discovers the story behind the invention of the hovercraft.

Morgan / Raleigh Super Grifter: The Restoring Classics team set out on a Pre-War challenge with a 1939 three wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on.
Ford Fiesta/Malton Forest Rally: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i that's not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to the West Midlands and London, where finds include a potato oven and an old petrol pump.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Unfinished Portrait: Lucy Worsley examines the author's later life and discovers how personal happiness ushered in a golden age for her writing.
Shopping from Home.
Paddington to Ongar: Michael Portillo ventures deep underground onto London's newest railway - the Elizabeth Line - enjoying a smooth ride from Paddington to Tottenham Court Road.
Felixstowe to Norwich: Michael Portillo explores Felixstowe, which suffered catastrophic flooding in 1953, and in Norwich, he admires the Norman Cathedral.
Brandon to Cambridge: Michael Portillo explores RAF Lakenheath, the market town of Brandon on the border between Norfolk and Suffolk, and he ends his journey in Cambridge.
Derby to Hinckley: Michael Portillo journeys from the Midlands to the West Country, visiting Derby's famous 19th-century railway works and the Walkers crisps factory in Leicester.
Land Rover/Dino/Morris 8/Saab 9-5: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree.
Consort Bike/Coble Boat/Volvo: Paul's on the East Coast to help a collector who is saying goodbye to his prized Excelsior Consort and his handmade Yorkshire Coble boat.
On Our Way: Recalling America's entry into World War II after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, the great Laurence Olivier sheds more light on the deadliest conflict in history.

programmes.show_name: True Evil: The Making Of A Nazi
programmes.show_description: Josef Goebbels: A profile of Josef Goebbels, the failed academic who channelled his resentment into a hatred of Jewish people, and enthusiastically led the Nazi campaign of terror.
Belmont House 1: Fiona Bruce presents from the grounds of Belmont House in Kent, where she encounters a disguised Michael McIntyre preparing a prank for his Saturday night show.
Clissold Park 3: Fiona Bruce presents from Clissold Park in Stoke Newington. Treasures include ancient Peruvian pottery, an archive of textile designs, and Harold Wilson's pipe.
Citroen / Tractor: The team invest in a piece of French art this week - a 1971 Citroen DS21 Pallas.
MGC / Seicento: Derek picks out a super low mileage MGC and the team pays a little too much to secure it.
On Our Way: Recalling America's entry into World War II after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, the great Laurence Olivier sheds more light on the deadliest conflict in history.

programmes.show_name: This World: The Tea Trail with Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: Simon Reeve travels to East African countries Kenya and Uganda to meet some of the millions of people involved in producing tea and learn more about the processes involved.
Alvis/Morris/WWT: Charlie picks up an iconic 60s motor - a Ford Cortina Deluxe - and a 1970s Morris van brings a taste of Carry On Camping to Pickering.
Rollo Croftmaster Tractor: Derek brings a small army of military hardware to Thornton le Dale including a Studebaker Weasel tracked vehicle and a WWII-era Willys Jeep.
Citroen / Tractor: The team invest in a piece of French art this week - a 1971 Citroen DS21 Pallas.
